scenario: Junction Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 with 11 Spring Boot services handling 17000 jobs per hour and a mature configuration still scattered under camunda.client.zeebe.defaults. The team is renaming every key to the documented 8.8 worker namespace before its next release cycle.
question: Which target prefix does the 8.8 reference document for every legacy camunda.client.zeebe.defaults entry?

options.text: The 8.8 reference points every camunda.client.zeebe.defaults entry to its sibling under camunda.client.worker.defaults at the unified worker namespace.
The 8.8 reference points every camunda.client.zeebe.defaults entry to its sibling under camunda.client.worker.override at the unified worker namespace.
The 8.8 reference points every camunda.client.zeebe.defaults entry to its sibling under camunda.client.cloud.defaults at the unified worker namespace.
The 8.8 reference points every camunda.client.zeebe.defaults entry to its sibling under camunda.client.defaults at the unified worker namespace.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe.defaults table maps each property row by row to the matching property under camunda.client.worker.defaults in 8.8.

b.text: Incorrect. Override entries are keyed by job type; the defaults section migrates one-to-one to camunda.client.worker.defaults instead.

c.text: Incorrect. The camunda.client.cloud section is for SaaS connection details and does not contain a defaults subblock in the 8.8 reference.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no flat camunda.client.defaults section; the documented replacement prefix is camunda.client.worker.defaults in 8.8.

explanation: Every row in the camunda.client.zeebe.defaults section of the 8.8 reference maps to the same-named property under camunda.client.worker.defaults. The worker block is the unified home for global job worker defaults.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
